extra.reasoning:
This is a state machine with variables opted_out (bool), sent (bool), ticks (int in [0,6]); initial state opted_out=false, sent=false, ticks=0. The safety property is globally(opted_out implies not sent): no reachable state may have opted_out=true and sent=true simultaneously.

The only transitions that set sent:=true are t_send_allowed (guard: not opted_out — safe) and t_resume_glitch (guard: opted_out and ticks>=6 — UNSAFE, it sets sent:=true while opted_out is true). t_send_blocked and t_tick both set sent:=false.

On a send_attempt with opted_out=true and ticks>=6, two transitions are enabled: t_send_blocked (guard opted_out) and t_resume_glitch (guard opted_out and ticks>=6). The spec gives no explicit priority, so in this nondeterministic transition system t_resume_glitch may fire, producing opted_out=true AND sent=true, which violates the property.

Concrete reachable counterexample (8 events, within the 10-event bound):
1. optout: opted_out:=true, sent:=false (ticks=0)
2-7. tick x6: each increments ticks (capped at 6) and sets sent:=false. After six ticks, ticks=6, opted_out=true, sent=false.
8. send_attempt: guard of t_resume_glitch (opted_out and ticks>=6) holds, so sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true, sent=true.

This reachable state has opted_out=true with sent=true, falsifying globally(opted_out implies not sent). Property is violated.
